summary: HAKKIVEDA backend migration from emergentintegrations -> google-genai validated. 36/36 backend tests pass against external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. emergentintegrations module fully removed (import fails as expected). google-genai>=0.8.0 present in requirements.txt. Server imports cleanly. With GOOGLE_API_KEY empty, AI Chat and AI Quiz return graceful fallbacks (no 500). All non-AI features (Auth, Products, Cart, Wishlist, Addresses, Checkout/Orders w/ HKV prefix, Coupons TRIBAL10/AYUR20/HAKKI500, Reviews, Newsletter, Contact, full Admin suite incl. stats/orders/customers/coupons/newsletter/contact/reviews/blog, Admin Products/Coupons/Blog/Site-Content/Profile CRUD, Order status updates, Image upload+static serve+delete, Payments config) work correctly.

minor.issue: Accepts arbitrary Dict[str,Any] body without Pydantic validation — allows clients to inject any fields including extra keys. Same for /admin/coupons, /admin/blog, /admin/site-content, /admin/profile, /admin/orders/{id}/status. Non-blocking but recommend Pydantic models for input validation.
Chat history is stored in MongoDB but never returned by any GET endpoint. The history is grown indefinitely (no pruning) which will bloat the chat_history collection.

action_items: Optional: replace Dict[str, Any] bodies in /admin/* endpoints with Pydantic models for stricter validation.
Optional: cap chat_history persistence (e.g., keep last 20 turns per session) to prevent unbounded growth.
When GOOGLE_API_KEY is set in Railway/Hostinger, retest /api/chat and /api/quiz/submit end-to-end against real Gemini 2.5 Flash.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1169 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers (auth.py, products.py, cart.py, orders.py, admin.py, ai.py).
MongoDB _id properly excluded across all queries via projection {'_id': 0}. Good.

google-genai client correctly handled when GOOGLE_API_KEY is empty — `genai_client` is None, code raises RuntimeError inside try/except which falls back gracefully (verified for both /api/chat and /api/quiz/submit).
/api/quiz/submit fallback returns a single product (correct per spec) instead of crashing — verified.
/api/chat fallback returns the hardcoded WhatsApp message — verified.
Coupon constants (COUPONS dict) are mutated at runtime by admin CRUD — works but stateful in-memory mutation alongside DB persistence is duplicated logic; consider sourcing solely from db.coupons.
Admin auth correctly returns 403 for non-admin users; 401 for missing token. Verified.
Order ID format HKV<YYYYMMDD><6char uuid> verified.
Static uploads served via /api/static/uploads/... successfully reachable from external URL.
test_credentials: Admin: hakkiveda@gmail.com / Hakki@Admin2026. Test users created on the fly (testuser+<ts>@example.com / TestPass123!). NOTE: /app/memory/test_credentials.md does NOT exist — should be created by main agent for future testers.
seed_data_creation: Created throwaway users (testuser+<ts>, fp+<ts>), newsletter signups (news+<ts>), contact submissions (contact+<ts>), a TEST_Product, TEST_Blog post, TEST<ts> coupon (deleted after use), several COD orders for admin status testing, and one review. All test orders/users persist in db but are isolated by timestamp prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run with `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_hakkiveda_migration.py -n 0 -v`. Do NOT use default `-n 2` because session-scoped user_token fixture runs per-worker and second registration hits 'Email already registered'. Either keep -n 0 OR refactor user_token to use a per-worker unique email (incorporate worker id). All 36 tests passed against the external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL on Jan 2026 run.
rca of the issue: N/A — no production bugs found. Initial test run had 5 ERRORs due to pytest-xdist's 2-worker default in pytest.ini: the session-scoped register fixture executed once per worker process using the same TS, causing the second worker's registration to 400 with 'Email already registered'. Mitigation: ran with `-n 0` (serial) and all tests passed. Future fix: incorporate `worker_id` from pytest-xdist into the test email when running parallel.
